headline: Downtown Billings Added 25 New Businesses in 2025 Amid Record Growth Streak
summary: Downtown Billings welcomed 25 new businesses in 2025, continuing a multi-year growth trend tracked by the Downtown Billings Alliance. New openings included six retail shops, five health and beauty businesses, five service-based businesses, and four coffee shops and bakeries. Despite eight closures — including Wild Ginger and Buffalo Block — organizers reported nine additional businesses already planned for early 2026.

headline: Billings Police Report Violent Crime Down Following Increased Patrols
summary: The Billings Police Department reported a decrease in violent crime following a sustained increase in patrol activity, Chief Rich St. John told city council. The department's annual report showed homicides declined from 15 to 5 in the reporting period, with the department attributing the improvement to targeted enforcement in high-incident areas.

headline: Billings Crime Statistics Dashboard Tracks Offenses Citywide Since 2024
summary: The City of Billings launched an interactive crime statistics dashboard providing real-time mapping of reported offenses across the city. The tool allows residents to search crime reports by location, date, and type, covering incidents from January 2024 onward and available on both desktop and mobile devices.

headline: Intermountain Health Breaks Ground on New St. Vincent Regional Hospital in Billings
summary: Intermountain Health broke ground in June 2025 on a full-replacement St. Vincent Regional Hospital in Billings — described as a once-in-a-generation project set to redefine healthcare for the region. The new facility, designed by Layton Construction, will replace the current hospital and is slated to serve Montana, Wyoming, and the western Dakotas.

headline: Intermountain St. Vincent Unveils Design for New Billings Replacement Hospital
summary: Intermountain Health St. Vincent Regional Hospital unveiled detailed design plans in November 2024 for its full-replacement hospital in Billings. The new campus will provide comprehensive acute care services and is expected to break ground in spring 2025, replacing the current facility that has served the region for over 125 years.

headline: RiverStone Health Celebrates 50th Anniversary as Yellowstone County Public Health Anchor
summary: RiverStone Health marked its 50th anniversary as Yellowstone County's public health department and 40th anniversary as Montana's first Community Health Center in 2024. The organization serves approximately 300 patients daily across medical, behavioral health, dental, rural clinics, and school-based health centers, with about one in five patients living below the poverty level.

headline: Rocky Mountain College Battlin' Bears Athletics Posts 3.19 Department GPA in Fall 2024
summary: Rocky Mountain College Athletics in Billings celebrated a 3.19 department GPA and 3.22 cumulative GPA following the Fall 2024 semester, with 128 student-athletes earning academic honors. Women's Cross Country led all programs with a 3.63 team GPA, and 28 Battlin' Bears were named Fall NAIA Daktronics Scholar-Athletes.

headline: Montana State University Billings Yellowjackets Compete in GNAC Conference Play 2024-25
summary: Montana State University Billings competes in 16 varsity sports as a member of the Great Northwest Athletic Conference (GNAC). The Yellowjackets field teams in men's and women's basketball, cross country, track and field, soccer, volleyball, softball, and golf, representing the primary four-year athletic program for the Billings metro area.

headline: Intermountain Health Becomes Title Sponsor of Billings' Premier Arena, Rebrands Venue
summary: Intermountain Health's $950 million hospital campus investment in Billings underscores the health system's deep commitment to the region, which includes naming partnerships with local venues and community sports programming. The Big Sky Economic Development's project tracker lists Intermountain Health as one of the largest economic investments in the city's history.

headline: KRTV Eye on Great Falls: New Businesses Open and Construction Underway in June 2024
summary: KRTV's monthly Eye on Great Falls feature for June 2024 highlighted a wave of new business openings, active construction projects, and several closed signs across the city. The report documented ongoing commercial activity in the downtown core and along major arterial corridors, reflecting broad economic momentum heading into summer.

headline: Grow Great Falls 2024 Downtown Market Assessment Shows Significant Commercial Momentum
summary: The Great Falls Development Authority's 2024 Downtown Market Assessment found significant momentum in downtown Great Falls, with year-round programming attracting diverse visitors and recent investments in housing and retail demonstrating growing demand. The report highlighted opportunities to concentrate more retail downtown and identified GFDA's financial tools as key supports for new business investment.

headline: Janicki Industries Selects Great Falls for $800 Million Aerospace Manufacturing Campus
summary: Washington state aerospace and defense manufacturer Janicki Industries selected Great Falls as the site for a new $800 million manufacturing campus, expected to bring more than 2,000 jobs to the region over several years. The announcement marked one of the largest industrial investments in Montana's history and was announced through the Montana Freepress.

headline: Great Falls Electric Basketball Team Enters Third Professional Season in 2026
summary: The Great Falls Electric, Montana's only professional basketball team and a member of The Basketball League (TBL), launched its third season in early 2026 under new head coach Broc Finstuen. After claiming the Western Conference championship in their second season, the Electric plays home games at Four Seasons Arena and has built a following since the franchise's 2024 debut.

headline: Thrive Cycle Indoor Cycling Studio Opens in Great Falls with SBDC Support
summary: Jennifer Renaud opened Thrive Cycle, a boutique indoor cycling studio, in Great Falls in 2023 after relocating from out of state. With help from the Great Falls SBDC, she developed a business plan and secured financing; the studio's classes regularly fill to capacity with waitlists forming for popular time slots.

headline: Great Falls Man Sentenced to More Than 10 Years on Federal Drug Charges
summary: Michael Shawn O'Neill, 59, a convicted felon from Great Falls, was sentenced in April 2025 to 128 months in federal prison followed by five years of supervised release after pleading guilty in December 2024 to possession with intent to distribute controlled substances. Federal prosecutors said he possessed both guns and drugs at the time of his arrest.
source_name: U.S. Attorney's Office, District of Montana

headline: Three Teens Charged in September 2024 Downtown Great Falls Assault That Left Man With Brain Injury
summary: Three teenagers were charged in connection with a September 2024 assault in a downtown Great Falls alley that left a man with a serious brain injury. The cases — involving a 14-year-old tried as an adult, a 19-year-old who pleaded guilty, and a third defendant — drew sustained attention in Cascade County courts through early 2025.

headline: KRTV Great Falls Tracks Multiple Charges Filed in Cascade County Courts in 2024-2025
summary: KRTV's crime and courts coverage documented a range of felony cases originating in Great Falls and Cascade County in 2024-2025, including charges of deliberate homicide, shots fired downtown, and multiple drug-related prosecutions. Reporters tracked cases from arrest through sentencing in district and municipal courts.

headline: Benefis Health System Delivers 1,347 Babies and Performs 8,708 Surgeries in 2024
summary: Benefis Health System's 2024 Spotlight Report documented a year of significant activity: 1,347 babies delivered, 8,708 surgeries performed, 297,477 outpatient clinic visits, and 30,390 emergency department visits. The Great Falls-based independent health system also launched its Epic electronic health record system and the MyChart patient app during 2024.

headline: Great Falls Clinic Invests $1.5 Million to Expand Cancer Infusion Suite
summary: The Great Falls Clinic announced in June 2024 a $1.5 million expansion of its Cancer Center Infusion Suite, unveiled at the "I AM: Beyond the Diagnosis" patient appreciation event. The remodel increases capacity for cancer patients receiving chemotherapy and other infusion-based treatments at the clinic's main campus.

headline: Great Falls Clinic Hospital Opens $58 Million North Tower, Wins Best Hospital Award for 2024
summary: The Great Falls Clinic Hospital completed its $58 million North Tower expansion, which added a 10-bed ICU, 18 new inpatient beds, a cardiac cath lab, two operating rooms, and expanded pharmacy and laboratory space. In January 2025, the facility was named Great Falls Community's Choice Best Hospital and Best Family Practice for 2024.

headline: Benefis Sletten Cancer Institute Provides Oncology Anchor for Montana's Hi-Line Region
summary: Benefis Sletten Cancer Institute in Great Falls serves as the primary comprehensive cancer care center for central and north-central Montana, offering medical oncology, radiation therapy, and supportive care. The institute's oncology team provides treatments including immunotherapy and chemotherapy while also offering palliative care and patient navigation services.

headline: Benefis Health Launches Helena Specialty Center and Spine Institute in 2024 Expansion
summary: As part of its 2024 expansion, Benefis Health System launched the Benefis Helena Specialty Center and began offering specialized care at the Benefis Spine Institute under board-certified orthopedic surgeon Dr. Anthony Russo. Great Falls Surgical Associates also joined the Benefis team during 2024, extending the system's surgical capacity.

headline: Great Falls Electric Win Western Conference Championship in Inaugural 2024-25 TBL Season
summary: In their inaugural 2024 season, the Great Falls Electric of The Basketball League made an immediate impact, winning the Western Conference championship and advancing to the league semifinals. Standout player Antwaan Cushingberry earned TBL Rookie of the Year honors as the team built a local following at Four Seasons Arena.

headline: Great Falls Electric Professional Basketball Franchise: First and Only in Montana Since 2024
summary: The Great Falls Electric hold the distinction of being Montana's first and only professional basketball franchise since their founding in 2024 as a member of The Basketball League. The team is dedicated to providing high-quality family entertainment, elite competition, and youth development programs for the Great Falls community.

headline: Great Falls High Bison Girls Basketball Builds on Multi-Year State Contender Status
summary: Great Falls High School's Bison girls basketball program has maintained competitive standing in Class AA Montana prep sports across recent seasons, tracking statistics through 2024-2025 on dedicated state sports archives. The Bison compete in the Western AA conference alongside rival schools from Missoula, Billings, and Kalispell.

headline: Great Falls Sees Business and Sports Development Momentum Heading into 2026
summary: Great Falls wrapped 2025 with notable momentum across both economic development and sports sectors, according to local reports. KRTV documented ongoing business growth, new restaurants, and expanded commercial activity across the city as the Great Falls Electric basketball franchise drew continued attention to the community.

headline: Great Falls High Bison Athletics Compete Across Full Slate of MHSA Sports in 2024
summary: Great Falls High School fields competitive programs in football, basketball, volleyball, softball, soccer, wrestling, and track and field, competing in Montana High School Association Class AA. The Bison athletics program operates under GFPS Athletic Director Mike Henneberg and serves hundreds of student-athletes annually.

headline: Bozeman Health and Billings Clinic Announce Co-Location Services at West Bozeman Facility
summary: Billings Clinic and Bozeman Health announced in June 2024 that Bozeman Health would lease space on one floor of Billings Clinic Bozeman's outpatient clinical facility at 3905 Wellness Way in west Bozeman. The arrangement keeps the two independent practices under one roof while each maintains distinct operations, improving patient convenience.

headline: New Downtown Bozeman Businesses Including Provecho and Tres Toros Open in 2025
summary: A wave of new businesses opened in downtown Bozeman in 2025, including Provecho (a Mexican brick-and-mortar inside the Bozeman Hotel), Tres Toros (a Big Sky favorite coming to the old Shred Monk spot), Burson & Company (an outdoor lifestyle shop), and Montana Light Lounge (a wellness spot on S. Willson). The cluster of openings reflected sustained retail demand in the city's core.

headline: DEA-Led Gallatin County Drug Bust Results in Multiple Federal Arrests in May 2026
summary: A large-scale, multi-location law enforcement operation in May 2026 targeting illegal drug trafficking in Gallatin County led to multiple arrests on federal criminal charges. The Missouri River Drug Task Force, Gallatin County Sheriff's Office, Bozeman Police Department, Montana DCI, and other state and federal agencies jointly executed search warrants at several locations across the Bozeman area.

headline: Missouri River Drug Task Force Investigates Trafficking Networks Operating in Gallatin County
summary: The Missouri River Drug Task Force — a multijurisdictional unit covering seven southwestern Montana counties — regularly targets drug distribution networks operating in and around Bozeman. The task force uses undercover surveillance, electronic monitoring, and federal partnerships with the DEA and U.S. Attorney's Office to disrupt drug organizations.

headline: Bozeman Health Opens Adult Inpatient Psychiatric Unit, First in the City's History
summary: Bozeman Health opened a new Adult Inpatient Psychiatric Unit at Deaconess Regional Medical Center in September 2025, filling a long-standing gap in the region. The unit, with a construction budget exceeding $6.5 million and a staff of more than 30 professionals, provides the only crisis care inpatient psychiatric facility for Gallatin County. In 2024, the health system completed 1,359 mental health crisis evaluations.

headline: Bozeman Health Adopts 2024 Subarea Plan to Guide Future Hospital Campus Growth
summary: The City of Bozeman officially adopted the 2024 Bozeman Health Subarea Plan in August 2024, reserving space for future growth of medical and commercial services supporting the regional medical center. The plan balances development with community priorities while ensuring Bozeman Health can continue meeting regional acute care needs.

headline: Bozeman Health Psychiatric Walk-In Clinic Provides Same-Day Mental Health Evaluations
summary: Bozeman Health's Outpatient Psychiatry Clinic offers a walk-in service available Monday through Friday where patients can receive same-day evaluations by a psychiatrist without an appointment. Navigator services help connect patients to outpatient psychiatry, counseling, and primary care, addressing Gallatin County's high demand for mental health services.

headline: MSU Bobcats Go 11-1 in 2024 Regular Season, Reach FCS Championship Game
summary: Montana State's 2024 football season produced an 11-1 regular season record including a 34-11 win over rival Montana in the Brawl of the Wild. The Bobcats advanced through FCS playoffs, defeating UT Martin, Idaho, and South Dakota before falling 32-35 to North Dakota State in the national championship game in Frisco, Texas.

headline: MTPR Reports Organizers Pitch Uptown Butte Business Improvement District in 2024
summary: Community organizers in Uptown Butte launched a campaign in June 2024 to establish a Business Improvement District, going door-to-door asking property owners to support a special tax fund for beautification, street maintenance, and marketing. Organizers targeted 60 percent of property owners within the proposed district to advance the proposal to a county commission vote.

headline: Uptown Butte Business Improvement District Established to Revitalize Historic Downtown
summary: The Uptown Butte Business Improvement District was formally established to support revitalization of Butte's historic uptown neighborhood, bringing together business owners, property managers, and community members. The BID is dedicated to enhancing the district's historic character, supporting local enterprises, and fostering economic growth in what advocates call one of Montana's most distinctive downtowns.

headline: Montana Headwaters Tech Hub Wins $41 Million Federal Grant, Butte Among Beneficiary Communities
summary: The Montana Headwaters Tech Hub Consortium received a $41 million grant from the U.S. Economic Development Administration in July 2024 to develop smart photonic sensing systems and autonomous technologies. Butte Local Development Corporation is a consortium member, and Montana Tech's Energy, Environment & Innovation Field Campus is developing a drone and robotics test bed funded by the award.

headline: Montana Connections Business Development Park in Butte Attracts Industrial Tenants
summary: Montana Connections Business Development Park in Butte continues to market its industrial sites to companies seeking a central Montana location with interstate access and proximity to an airport. The park promotes Butte's cost advantages, skilled manufacturing workforce, and its history in engineering and processing to attract manufacturers and technology companies.

headline: Montana Standard Tracks Crime and Courts Beat Across Butte-Silver Bow County
summary: The Montana Standard's crime and courts coverage in Butte documented a wide range of cases in 2024-2025, including sentencing in a 2008 bounty-hunting shooting death case and multiple felony cases proceeding through Butte-Silver Bow district courts. The publication serves as the primary newspaper of record for local law enforcement and judicial proceedings.

headline: Intermountain Health Names New President of St. James Hospital in Butte for 2024
summary: Intermountain Health announced in March 2024 that Karen Costello would become the new President of Intermountain Health St. James Hospital in Butte, effective April 1, 2024. St. James is a 67-bed acute care facility serving Southwestern Montana for nearly 145 years, with more than 450 caregivers and about 100 physicians.

headline: St. James Hospital Earns Outstanding Patient Experience Award in 2024 for Third Consecutive Year
summary: Intermountain Health St. James Hospital in Butte received the Healthgrades Outstanding Patient Experience Award for 2024, marking the third consecutive year the facility earned the top-tier designation. The award recognizes patient experience scores 8 percent higher than the national average across measures including doctor communication, hospital cleanliness, and post-discharge care.

headline: St. James Hospital Supports Youth Behavioral Health Through Hope Squads in Butte Schools
summary: Intermountain Health St. James Hospital provided funding and support in 2025 to establish Hope Squads at five Butte secondary schools, peer-led groups trained in suicide prevention. The hospital also funded community partners including Southwest Montana Community Health Center and Butte Cares, distributing 6,526 educational items and reaching more than 1,500 elementary students through wellness assemblies.

headline: Montana Tech Orediggers Men's Basketball Wins Fourth Straight Frontier Conference Title in 2024-25
summary: The Montana Tech Orediggers men's basketball program made history in 2024-25 by winning their fourth consecutive Frontier Conference regular season and tournament championship, the first men's basketball program to achieve the feat. The Orediggers reached the highest national ranking in program history at No. 5 and set a school record as the fastest team to 20 wins in a season.

headline: Montana Tech Football Goes 9-3 in 2024, Reaches NAIA Playoffs
summary: Montana Tech's 2024 football season produced a 9-3 record, with wins over Carroll College, Eastern Oregon, Arizona Christian, and MSU-Northern. The Orediggers hosted a first-round NAIA playoff game before falling to Northwestern (Iowa) 29-32, capping another successful season for the Frontier Conference program.

headline: Montana Tech Wins Bandy Award for All-Sports Excellence, First Since 1993
summary: Montana Tech won the Frontier Conference's prestigious Bandy Award for all-sports excellence in 2023, the first time since 1993. The award recognized the Orediggers' sustained success across multiple intercollegiate sports, reflecting broad-based athletic achievement at the Butte university.

headline: Montana Awarded $41 Million for Headwaters Technology Hub Focused on Smart Photonic Sensing
summary: The U.S. Economic Development Administration awarded Montana's Headwaters Tech Hub $41 million in July 2024 to grow a globally leading technology ecosystem anchored in smart photonic sensing systems. One of only 12 Tech Hubs funded nationally from more than 300 applications, the award is expected to drive economic development across Montana including in Helena.

headline: Governor Gianforte Announces Record 63,814 New Business Registrations in Montana in 2024
summary: Governor Greg Gianforte and Secretary of State Christi Jacobsen announced in January 2025 that Montanans registered a record 63,814 new businesses in 2024, surpassing the previous record by nearly 4,000. The announcement was made at Granite Peak Athletics in East Helena, one of the thousands of new businesses created during the year.

headline: Granite Peak Athletics Opens in East Helena With SBDC Support, Recognized at State Event
summary: Granite Peak Athletics LLC, a baseball and softball training facility co-founded by Connor Moore and Nolan Gilbert in East Helena, opened with assistance from the Helena SBDC. The business received financing through SBDC guidance and was honored during a Governor's event in 2025 celebrating Montana's record business creation year.

headline: Benefis Health Opens Helena Specialty Center and Spine Institute in 2024 Expansion
summary: As part of its 2024 growth, Great Falls-based Benefis Health System launched the Benefis Helena Specialty Center and began offering specialized spine care under board-certified orthopedic surgeon Dr. Anthony Russo. Great Falls Surgical Associates also joined the Benefis team during the year, expanding the system's surgical presence in the capital city.

headline: Helena Independent Record Tracks Active Crime and Courts Beat in 2024-2025
summary: The Helena Independent Record's crime and courts coverage documented numerous felony cases in Lewis and Clark County during 2024-2025, including drug distribution sentences, sexual assault prosecutions, and a fatal multi-crash spike in late April that saw 12 fatalities across 10 crashes in nine days along Montana highways. The paper serves as the newspaper of record for Helena-area law enforcement.

headline: St. Peter's Health in Helena Transitions to Epic Electronic Health Record System
summary: St. Peter's Health in Helena announced a transition to Epic, a best-in-class electronic health record system, with a system-wide launch beginning June 10 at the St. Peter's Health Regional campus. The upgrade modernizes the hospital's patient data infrastructure and improves coordination across the 24-bed behavioral center and 99-bed acute care facility.

headline: St. Peter's Health Cancer Care Offers TrueBeam Radiation Therapy at Helena Campus
summary: St. Peter's Health Cancer Care in Helena provides advanced cancer treatment including immunotherapy, chemotherapy, and radiation therapy using the TrueBeam linear accelerator. The outpatient cancer care center is located at the Maria Dean Building on East Broadway, with an oncology inpatient unit for patients requiring hospital-level care.

headline: St. Peter's Health Donates Decommissioned Ambulance to Helena College for EMS Training
summary: St. Peter's Health donated a decommissioned ambulance to Helena College to strengthen emergency medical services workforce training in the Helena area. The donation supports Helena College's EMS program and reflects the hospital's commitment to building local healthcare capacity beyond its own walls.

headline: Carroll College Fighting Saints Football Posts 7-3 Record in 2024 Frontier Conference Season
summary: Carroll College's Fighting Saints football team went 7-3 in 2024, defeating Rocky Mountain College, Simpson, Arizona Christian, MSU-Northern, Eastern Oregon, Southern Oregon, and College of Idaho. The Saints hosted all home games at Nelson Stadium in Helena and competed under head coach Troy Purcell in the NAIA Frontier Conference.

headline: Carroll College Fighting Saints Announce 2024 Signing Class for Football Program
summary: Carroll College football announced its 2024 signing class in February 2024, adding recruits from Montana and surrounding states to the program coached by Troy Purcell. Carroll competes in 15 NAIA varsity sports, with the football program having a storied tradition as one of the most successful in NAIA history.

headline: Logan Health Medical Center Named Top 100 Rural and Community Hospital for 2024
summary: Logan Health Medical Center in Kalispell was recognized as a 2024 Top 100 Rural & Community Hospital by The Chartis Center for Rural Health, based on the Chartis Rural Hospital Performance INDEX. The 192-bed acute care regional referral center is the flagship of Logan Health, serving northwest Montana and offering a full spectrum of healthcare services.

headline: Glacier Precast: Kalispell-Founded Concrete Manufacturer Behind Region's Infrastructure
summary: Glacier Precast, founded in 1989 in Kalispell, manufactures the concrete components — including septic systems and wastewater systems — that underpin regional development across northwest Montana. The company is highlighted in the Daily Inter Lake's Insights business journal as one of Kalispell's enduring manufacturing success stories.

headline: Bias Brewing Builds Community Through Craft Beer and Montana Agriculture in Downtown Kalispell
summary: Bias Brewing operates in downtown Kalispell, crafting beer using Montana agricultural products and building community around the brewery's taproom. The Daily Inter Lake's Insights feature highlighted Bias as emblematic of a broader craft beverage trend rooted in Montana's farm-to-glass movement.

headline: Discover Kalispell Chamber Hosts 120th Anniversary Grand Event in November 2024
summary: The Discover Kalispell Chamber celebrated its 120th birthday at the Hilton Garden Inn in November 2024, honoring award winners in categories including Small Business, Large Business, Non-profit, Community Spirit, and Rising Star of the Year. The annual Grand Event also serves as the Chamber Foundation's primary fundraiser.

headline: Stockman Bank Completes Conversion of Rocky Mountain Bank Branches in Kalispell, Bigfork, and Plains
summary: Stockman Bank completed the conversion of Rocky Mountain Bank branches in Kalispell, Bigfork, and Plains in July 2024, with systems converted and new signage in place over a single weekend. CEO Bill Coffee welcomed Rocky Mountain Bank employees and customers to the Stockman family as the conversion expanded the bank's northwest Montana footprint.

headline: Kalispell City Council Revokes Flathead Warming Center Permit in 2024, Triggering Landmark Lawsuit
summary: The Kalispell City Council voted in 2024 to revoke the Flathead Warming Center's permit to operate, citing concerns from neighbors and businesses about crime and the homeless shelter's impact on the area. The shelter sued the city, with its attorney arguing the case could set a national precedent; a judge later allowed the shelter to reopen pending the outcome.

headline: Kalispell Voters Asked to Approve Public Safety Levy to Expand Police Department
summary: Facing staffing shortages and an 86 percent increase in violent crime from 2018 to 2022, Kalispell officials placed a public safety levy on a March 2024 mail-in ballot. The measure asked voters to authorize 60.9 additional mills annually — approximately $369 per $450,000 of assessed value — to add detectives, a crime analyst, and additional patrol officers.

headline: Kalispell City Council Updates Public Participation Process After State Law Shifts Subdivision Approvals
summary: Kalispell City Council moved in mid-2026 to update its public participation process after Montana's Land Use Planning Act eliminated public hearings for certain subdivision approvals. The city created a new online portal for development applications, email notification subscriptions, and maintained written public comment windows for residents to engage with development proposals.

headline: Kalispell City Airport Receives $556,395 Montana Aeronautics Grant for Taxiway Replacement
summary: Kalispell received a $556,395 Montana Aeronautics Board grant in September 2025 for taxiway replacement at Kalispell City Airport. The City Council subsequently considered hiring LHC Inc. for $350,000 to carry out the taxiway rip-up and replacement project, with the grant covering the majority of the cost.

headline: Four Found Dead in Kalispell Home in Apparent Murder-Suicide in 2024
summary: Kalispell police responded to a mental health call in May 2024 and found four people dead from apparent gunshot wounds at a home in the Silverbrook Estates area. Investigators determined the incident was an isolated murder-suicide involving Nicholas Olson-Hartley, who had made concerning statements to 911 dispatchers; two of the victims were young children.

headline: Whitefish Man Sentenced to 30 Years for Deliberate Homicide at Apartment Complex
summary: A Flathead County jury convicted Steven Justin Hedrick in January 2024 of deliberate homicide for the 2023 fatal shooting of his neighbor at Locals Monthly Lodging in Whitefish. District Court Judge Amy Eddy sentenced Hedrick to 30 years in Montana State Prison with no time suspended at an April 4, 2024 hearing.

headline: Kalispell Police Department Response Time Averages 9.1 Minutes Amid Growing City
summary: A public safety audit of the Kalispell Police Department found the agency's average response time was 9.1 minutes — significantly higher than the national average of five minutes — as Kalispell's population grew 15.8 percent between 2020 and 2023 to 28,450 residents. The city's violent crime rate, while stable on a per-capita basis, showed absolute increases in reported incidents.

headline: Flathead County Tracks DUI, Theft, and Drug Cases Across Kalispell Jurisdictions in 2024
summary: Flathead County's active crime and courts docket in 2024 included DUI prosecutions, vehicle thefts, and drug cases processed through Flathead County District Court. The Flathead Beacon's police blotter coverage documented incidents ranging from shoplifting arrests to drug possession charges, reflecting the range of law enforcement activity across the valley.

headline: Kalispell Chief Participates in State Panel on Homelessness and Law Enforcement Challenges
summary: Kalispell Police Chief Jordan Venezio participated in a panel discussion at the 2024 Montana Crime Prevention Conference at Fairmont Hot Springs, addressing the intersection of homelessness and law enforcement across Montana cities. The panel included chiefs from Great Falls, Missoula, and Yellowstone County as panelists examined community policing responses to housing instability.

headline: Logan Health Named Top Hospital for Social Responsibility, Ranked No. 1 in Montana
summary: Logan Health Medical Center in Kalispell was named a Top Hospital for Social Responsibility by the Lown Institute for the second consecutive year, earning Honor Roll status with 'A' grades across all categories. The hospital ranked No. 1 in Montana for Social Responsibility, Clinical Outcomes, and Community Benefit — and 37th nationally among more than 2,700 acute care hospitals.

headline: Logan Health Expands Prostate Cancer Care With PLUVICTO Targeted Treatment in Kalispell
summary: Logan Health in Kalispell became the third health system in Montana to offer PLUVICTO, a targeted radioligand therapy for prostate cancer, administered at Logan Health Medical Center's Infusion Center. Unlike traditional chemotherapy, PLUVICTO attacks prostate cancer cells directly with fewer systemic side effects, and the first treatment was administered in September 2025.

headline: Logan Health Operates SANE Program Serving Sexual Assault Survivors Across Northwest Montana
summary: Logan Health Medical Center in Kalispell operates one of the few 24-hour sexual assault nurse examiner (SANE) programs in Montana, serving a vast region stretching from Polson and Libby to Eureka and communities along the Hi-Line. Logan Health is working toward a tele-SANE program to extend support to rural hospitals statewide.

headline: Logan Health Transitions to Oracle Health EHR System Across 30 Billings Clinic-Logan Health Hospitals
summary: Billings Clinic and Logan Health announced in October 2024 an expanded deployment of Oracle Health's electronic health record system across all 30 of their combined hospitals. The integrated EHR is expected to improve patient access to coordinated care and deliver operational efficiencies across the merged Montana health system.

headline: Logan Health Launches "Let's Talk About It" Mental Health Campaign to Reduce Suicide in Montana
summary: Logan Health and Billings Clinic jointly launched the "Let's Talk About It" campaign to dismantle the stigma surrounding mental health in Montana, where suicide rates have remained among the nation's highest for over 40 years. The initiative uses community education and open dialogue to unite eastern and western Montana around shared mental health challenges.

headline: Glacier Wolfpack Girls Flag Football Wins Third Consecutive State Championship in 2024
summary: The Glacier High School girls flag football team captured their third straight state championship in 2024, winning an 18-13 final after advancing through a competitive two-day tournament. Head coach Mark Hestler praised the team's resilience after a challenging tournament bracket, with the Wolfpack overcoming multiple opponents in the new format.

headline: Glacier High School Boys Soccer Earns Western AA Title in 2024, Eyes State Championship
summary: Glacier High School's boys soccer team won the Western AA title in 2024 with a strong regular season, highlighted by standout player Liam Ells earning Western AA Player of the Year honors. The Wolfpack were considered top contenders to bring home a state championship, supported by a deep roster of seniors and experienced underclassmen.

headline: Glacier Wolfpack Golf Teams Sweep Western AA Championships at Canyon River Golf Club
summary: Glacier High School's golf teams swept the Western AA championships, with Toren Murray winning the individual boys title in a playoff at Canyon River Golf Club. The Wolfpack girls also won the overall team competition, while Glacier's cross country runners Owen Theel and Lauren Bon each captured individual titles at the Mountain West Classic.

headline: Glacier Wolfpack Football Dominates 2024 Season, Defeats Billings West in Season Opener
summary: The Glacier High School Wolfpack opened the 2025 football season with a 14-10 road win over the No. 1-ranked Billings West Golden Bears, signaling another strong year for one of Montana's top Class AA programs. Glacier, which won its first football state championship in 2014, plays home games at Legends Stadium in Kalispell.

headline: Flathead Valley Soccer Preview 2024: Six Straight Class A Finals Won by Valley Teams
summary: For six consecutive years through 2024, a team from the Flathead Valley won the Class A state soccer final, with Whitefish Bulldogs capturing four titles and Columbia Falls winning twice. The 2024 preview highlighted Whitefish, Columbia Falls, Glacier, and Flathead as top contenders across both Class A and Class AA state competitions.

headline: Independence Bank Promotes Christy Smith to Assistant VP of Retail Banking in Havre
summary: Independence Bank announced the promotion of Christy Smith to Assistant Vice President of Retail Banking and Processing, recognizing nearly two decades of service that began when she joined the bank as a Collections Officer in January 2007. The promotion reflects the Hi-Line community bank's continued investment in local talent.

headline: Dunham's Sports Opens New Havre Store in September 2024
summary: Dunham's Sports announced the grand opening of its Havre store on Friday, September 13, 2024, bringing a major sporting goods retailer to the Hi-Line community. The chain invited Havre residents to experience its wide selection of name-brand sporting equipment at competitive prices.

headline: Creative Yogurt Adds Creative Coffee in January 2024, Expanding Havre Business
summary: Havre locals Nicole and Andy Hanson opened Creative Coffee on January 2, 2024, as an addition to their established Creative Yogurt business. The renovated space now offers both yogurt and coffee services, with the Hansons saying the two concepts have complemented each other well since the expansion.

headline: Frankie's Coffee Shop Opens Inside Havre's Atrium Mall
summary: Frankie's, a coffee shop owned by husband-wife team Frankie and Travis Chamberlain, opened on the main floor of Havre's Atrium Mall. The Chamberlains, described as elementary school sweethearts, have brought a new dining option to the mall's tenant mix as the Hi-Line's retail landscape continues to evolve.

headline: Tilleman Equipment Acquires Frontier Precision Agriculture to Expand Hi-Line Ag Tech Services
summary: Tilleman Equipment, a leading agricultural machinery provider in Montana, acquired Frontier Precision Agriculture — formerly Triangle Ag Services — to strengthen its precision agriculture offerings across the Hi-Line region. The acquisition adds advanced GPS guidance systems, crop monitoring drones, and related technology services to Tilleman's existing equipment portfolio.

headline: Wade Bitz Sworn In as Havre Mayor in January 2026
summary: Wade Bitz was inaugurated as Havre's new mayor in January 2026 after winning election in November on a platform focused on community engagement and accessibility to residents. Bitz indicated the city would move forward on tax increment financing district improvements and infrastructure upgrades during his term.

headline: Havre City Council Considers Non-Essential Water Use Restrictions Ordinance
summary: The Havre City Council advanced Ordinance 934 (second reading) to adopt non-essential water use monitoring and restrictions, addressing the city's water management needs during peak demand periods. The measure was part of a broader set of municipal issues handled at council's regular monthly meetings held the first Monday of each month.

headline: Havre City-County Airport Board Meets Regularly to Oversee Hill County Aviation Services
summary: The Havre City-County Airport Board meets monthly at City Hall to oversee operations at Havre's regional airport, which connects the Hi-Line community to Montana's broader air transportation network. The board coordinates with state aviation authorities and city government on infrastructure improvements and service continuity.

headline: Hill County Commission Seeks Applicants for Multiple Open Board Positions in 2024
summary: The Hill County Commission released an updated list of open board positions in mid-2024, seeking applicants for the Zoning Board of Adjustments, Great Northern Fair Commission, Hill County Planning Board, Historic Preservation Commission, and Tourism Business Improvement District among others. The open seats reflected ongoing community governance needs across Havre and Hill County.

headline: Great Northern Fair Commission Plans Annual Hi-Line Agricultural Fair in Havre
summary: The Great Northern Fair Board meets regularly to plan Havre's annual summer agricultural fair, one of the signature events on the Hi-Line calendar. The fair features carnival rides, livestock shows, 4-H competitions, and local entertainment, drawing visitors from across north-central Montana each July.

headline: Havre Meth and Fentanyl Trafficker Sentenced to 10 Years in Federal Prison
summary: Lance Jon Stimson, 33, of Havre was sentenced in January 2025 to 10 years in federal prison and five years of supervised release after pleading guilty in October 2024 to conspiring to distribute methamphetamine and fentanyl. FBI investigators linked Stimson to the distribution of more than 7,000 fentanyl pills and 17 ounces of methamphetamine between October 2023 and April 2024.
source_name: U.S. Attorney's Office, District of Montana

headline: Havre Police Department Among Montana's Larger Municipal Agencies With 18 Sworn Officers
summary: The Havre Police Department, led by Chief Gabe Matosich, is the seventh-largest municipal police department in Montana with 18 sworn officers. The department is joined in Havre by the Hill County Sheriff's Department, Montana Highway Patrol, U.S. Border Patrol Sector Headquarters, FBI, and the Tri-Agency Drug Task Force.

headline: Havre Public Schools Hit by Ransomware Attack Knocking Out Computers for a Week
summary: Havre Public Schools' computer system was infected with a ransomware virus in early February, knocking out district computers for approximately one week. The attack highlighted cybersecurity vulnerabilities in rural school districts and prompted discussions about improved digital infrastructure protection across the Hi-Line.

headline: Tri-Agency Drug Task Force Maintains Federal Partnership to Combat Hi-Line Narcotics
summary: The Tri-Agency Drug Task Force, based in Havre, operates as a collaborative law enforcement unit combining resources from the Havre Police Department, Hill County Sheriff, and federal partners to combat drug trafficking in north-central Montana. The task force coordinates with the FBI's Montana Regional Violent Crime Task Force on cases with multi-jurisdictional dimensions.

headline: Havre Sees Racially Disparate Arrest Patterns for Low-Level Offenses, Report Finds
summary: A Police Scorecard analysis of the Havre Police Department found that Black residents were 28.5 times more likely and Latinx residents were 1.7 times more likely than white residents to be arrested for low-level, non-violent offenses. The report, covering 2013-2023, documented 10,752 total arrests, with 60 percent classified as low-level, non-violent offenses.

headline: Montana Actors' Theatre Presents Hill Country Revue Variety Show in Havre in 2024
summary: The Montana Actors' Theatre produced the Hill Country Revue Variety Show in January and February 2024 at Cowan Hall on MSU-Northern's campus, inspired by Hee-Haw, SNL, and Rowan & Martin's Laugh-In. The show featured local musicians playing original music alongside comic sketches commenting on Hi-Line life, with weekend performances running through February.

headline: Havre Festival Days 2024 Returns with Car Show, Parade, and Softball Tournament in September
summary: Havre's Festival Days returned September 20-22, 2024, filling downtown with a Car Show on Friday evening, a Saturday parade at 10 a.m., KNMC Tribute Band concerts at Town Square, a vendors and crafts show at Holiday Village Mall, and a 48-hour softball tournament. The annual community celebration organized by the Havre Area Chamber of Commerce is one of the Hi-Line's signature events.

headline: Sounds on the Square Summer Concert Series Brings Weekly Live Music to Havre Town Square
summary: Havre's Sounds on the Square concert series brings free Wednesday evening performances to Town Square each summer, featuring local and regional acts. The 2026 lineup included bands like The Highliners, All Sorts, Gene Allen & Friends, and Plowed Under, continuing a tradition that draws hundreds of community members downtown each week.

headline: Backyard Storytellers Acoustic Concert Series at Spruce & Steel Music in Havre
summary: Spruce & Steel Music, a family-owned instrument shop in Havre, hosts the Backyard Storytellers acoustic concert series featuring touring singer-songwriters in an intimate setting. The series pairs original music performances with food from Guard Dogs hot dogs and Tart Pastry, with Vic's Place providing a cash bar — offering Havre an intimate live music option year-round.

headline: Visit Havre Highlights Great Northern Fair, Rocky Boy Pow Wow Among Annual Signature Events
summary: The Visit Havre tourism website highlights a calendar of annual events that draw visitors to the Hi-Line, including the Great Northern Fair in mid-July (featuring carnival rides and livestock shows), the Rocky Boy Pow Wow in early August celebrating Native American culture, and Festival Days in mid-September. These recurring events anchor Havre's community calendar year-round.

headline: Northern Montana Hospital: 25-Bed Critical Access Facility at the Heart of the Hi-Line
summary: Northern Montana Hospital, a 25-bed Critical Access Hospital in Havre, serves as the healthcare hub for patients across Montana's Hi-Line and beyond. Part of Northern Montana Health Care, the facility offers inpatient and outpatient services, cardiopulmonary rehabilitation, long-term care, and emergency care at its campus on 13th Street.

headline: Bullhook Community Health Center Serves Havre and Hi-Line as Federally Qualified Health Center
summary: Bullhook Community Health Center (BCHC) at 521 4th Street in Havre is a federally qualified health center serving Hill County and surrounding areas regardless of insurance or ability to pay. BCHC offers medical, dental, pharmacy, addiction counseling, mental health (including psychiatry telehealth), and school-based prevention services at Havre Public Schools.

headline: Northern Montana Health Care Expands Specialty Services and Cardiac Rehab Across Hi-Line
summary: Northern Montana Health Care provides cardiopulmonary rehabilitation and a range of specialty and outpatient services at its Havre campus, meeting the needs of a geographically isolated population. The organization's multiple departments serve patients from across north-central Montana who otherwise face multi-hour drives to reach specialist care.

headline: Logan Health Outreach Specialists Travel to Hi-Line Communities Including Havre-Area Clinics
summary: Logan Health specialists from Kalispell make regular trips to outreach sites across the Hi-Line region, providing cardiology, orthopedics, endocrinology, pediatric care, and approximately 30 other specialties to rural communities. The outreach program serves patients who cannot easily travel to Kalispell and has shown improved outcomes and patient satisfaction at Hi-Line clinic sites.

headline: Enell Sports Bras: Havre-Based Women's Activewear Company with National Reach
summary: Enell Sports Bras, founded by Renelle Braaten, is a successful womenswear company based in Havre that has grown into a nationally recognized brand. As a small-city manufacturer with a global customer base, Enell demonstrates that Hi-Line businesses can compete nationally while remaining rooted in north-central Montana.

headline: Bear Paw Marathon Returns to Havre's Bear Paw Mountains for Annual June Race
summary: The Bear Paw Marathon, held annually in June in Havre, takes runners through the scenic Bear Paw Mountains and Beaver Creek Park — the largest county park in the nation. The race offers marathon, half-marathon, and 5K distances and draws participants from across the region to experience the unique island mountain terrain south of Havre.

headline: MSU-Northern Lights Basketball Hosts Rocky Mountain College in February 2024 Home Game
summary: MSU-Northern's Lights and Skylights basketball teams hosted Rocky Mountain College in February 2024 at the MSU-Northern Armory Gym, competing in Frontier Conference play. MSU-Northern fields both men's and women's basketball programs along with football, golf, wrestling, volleyball, cross country, and rodeo as part of its NAIA athletic program.

headline: MSU-Northern Athletics Fields Seven Varsity Sports at NAIA Level for Havre Students
summary: Montana State University-Northern fields competitive teams in basketball, football, golf, wrestling, volleyball, cross country, and rodeo as part of its NAIA athletics program in Havre. The Lights and Skylights compete in the Frontier Conference and Cascade Collegiate Conference, representing Havre on a regional and national stage.

headline: Hi-Line Invitational Tournament Brings College Basketball to MSU-Northern in March 2024
summary: The Hi-Line Invitational Tournament (H.I.T.) brought college basketball action to the MSU-Northern Armory Gym over multiple days in March 2024, serving as a marquee event on Havre's sports calendar. The tournament draws teams from the region and provides an opportunity for the community to watch competitive collegiate basketball in the Hi-Line.

headline: MSU-Northern Football Falls to Montana Tech 54-14 in 2024 Frontier Conference Game
summary: MSU-Northern's football team fell to rival Montana Tech 54-14 on November 9, 2024, in a Frontier Conference matchup at Alumni Coliseum in Butte. The Lights compete in the NAIA Frontier Conference, playing home games in Havre while drawing students and community members together around their athletic programs.

headline: Whitefish Develops Economic Growth Policy Update in 2024 to Manage Rapid Expansion
summary: The City of Whitefish engaged stakeholders in 2024 through the Whitefish Economic Growth Policy Update process, addressing how to manage the rapidly growing tourism-based economy. With more than 800 locally owned small businesses, Whitefish faces ongoing challenges balancing growth, workforce housing, and community character.

headline: Ski-In Ski-Out Powder Peak Homes Break Ground at Whitefish Mountain Resort
summary: Developers held an official groundbreaking event for new Powder Peak ski-in/ski-out homes at Whitefish Mountain Resort, built on the site of the former Alpinglow Inn. Phase 1 includes 10 units near Chairs 1 and 2, each with private hot tubs, heated garages, and mountain views, with prices starting at $2.5 million.

headline: Jury Convicts Whitefish Man of Deliberate Homicide in January 2024 Trial
summary: A Flathead County District Court jury convicted Steven Justin Hedrick in January 2024 of felony deliberate homicide for the 2023 shooting death of neighbor Jeffery Brookshire at Locals Monthly Lodging in Whitefish. The jury deliberated for more than six hours before returning the verdict, with sentencing set for March 7.

headline: Venezuelan Legal Resident Arrested by Border Patrol in Whitefish During Traffic Stop, Later Released
summary: A 33-year-old Venezuelan man who had legally entered the United States was arrested by Border Patrol in Whitefish in April 2025 after a traffic stop escalated to federal involvement. Beker Rengifo del Castillo was held for nearly a week before being released from an ICE detention facility in Tacoma, Washington. The incident prompted community protests at the Whitefish Border Patrol station and criticism of local police for involving federal agents in a routine stop.

headline: Two Arrested in Whitefish After Reported Burglary and Multiple Vehicle Thefts in April 2025
summary: Whitefish police arrested two people in April 2025 after a reported burglary and theft of multiple vehicles in the East Edgewood area of Whitefish. Officers spotted one of the stolen vehicles and attempted a traffic stop, but suspects fled before being apprehended by Whitefish Police.

headline: Whitefish Police Department Maintains Online Crime Reporting System for Non-Emergency Cases
summary: The Whitefish Police Department operates an online non-emergency crime reporting portal through MyPDConnect, allowing residents to file reports for qualifying incidents without calling dispatch. The Whitefish City Hall posts regular police blotter updates covering property crimes, vehicle incidents, and other non-violent cases in the resort community.

headline: Logan Health Whitefish Earns 5-Star Overall CMS Rating and Top Patient Experience Score
summary: Logan Health – Whitefish received a 5-star overall rating from the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services and a 5-star rating specifically for patient experience, as recognized in Logan Health's 2024 Report to the Community. The 25-bed Critical Access Hospital serves more than 30,000 people in Whitefish, Columbia Falls, and surrounding canyon communities.

headline: Logan Health Whitefish Launches Fall Prevention Project, Distributes Ice Cleats to High-Risk Residents
summary: Logan Health – Whitefish launched a Fall Prevention Project, distributing ice cleats to high fall-risk residents in Columbia Falls, Eureka, and Whitefish to reduce winter injury rates. The program targets older adults and those with mobility challenges in the mountain community, where icy conditions pose significant fall hazards.

headline: Logan Health Whitefish Begins Transition to Oracle Health PatientConnect Patient Portal
summary: Logan Health announced in September 2024 that it is transitioning to Oracle Health as its new electronic health record system, with Logan Health – Whitefish expected to adopt the new PatientConnect portal by early 2027 as part of a phased rollout. The transition replaces the current myHealth portal and aligns Whitefish with the broader Billings Clinic-Logan Health integrated system.

headline: Logan Health Whitefish Birth Center Delivers Over 1,197 Babies Across System in 2023
summary: Logan Health's Birth Center at Whitefish is among the facilities contributing to the health system's delivery of 1,197 babies across its six hospitals in 2023, as reported in the 2024 Community Report. Logan Health – Whitefish provides labor and delivery services for Flathead Valley families who prefer care closer to the mountain community.

headline: Logan Health Whitefish Provides Emergency Department Services 24 Hours a Day to Flathead Valley
summary: Logan Health – Whitefish operates a 24-hour emergency department accessible by calling 406-863-3528, offering full emergency services for adults and children in the Whitefish area. The facility's emergency services team collaborates with Logan Health's Level III trauma center at Kalispell for cases requiring higher-level trauma care.

headline: Whitefish Bulldogs Soccer Win Class A State Championship for Fourth Time, 10th Overall
summary: The Whitefish Bulldogs boys soccer team captured their 10th overall Class A state championship after going undefeated in the 2023 season, capping a remarkable run in which Whitefish has dominated the Class A soccer landscape in Montana. For four years out of six, the Bulldogs claimed the state trophy before Columbia Falls won back-to-back titles.

headline: Whitefish Bulldogs Seek Bond for New High School Athletic Facilities in September 2024
summary: Bulldogs United, a community advocacy committee, campaigned for a YES vote on two bond issues for Whitefish High School on September 17, 2024, to fund the expansion and upgrade of academic and athletic facilities. Organizers cited recent population growth and the need for facilities where Whitefish student-athletes can compete on equal footing with peers across Montana.

headline: State Identifies 114-Acre Site in Laurel for New $27.2 Million Forensic Mental Health Facility
summary: Montana officials identified a 114-acre plot along Old Highway 10 in west Laurel as the proposed location for a new $27.2 million forensic mental health facility. At a Laurel City Council meeting, the state's Board of Investments Director Dan Villa said Montana is in a buy-sell agreement for the land and would request annexation and zoning, with plans for a 32-bed facility serving justice-involved individuals awaiting mental competency evaluations.

headline: Laurel Brewfest Annual Fundraiser Supports Chamber Beautification and Business Community
summary: The Laurel Brewfest at Millers Horse Palace is the Laurel Chamber of Commerce's major annual fundraiser, featuring unlimited beer sampling from local breweries, food trucks, and live music. Proceeds support beautification projects, committee programs, and opportunities for Laurel's business community, with proceeds in recent years targeted at the Laurel Beautification Program.

headline: Laurel Residents React With Mixed Views as State Chooses Town for Forensic Mental Health Hospital
summary: Community members in Laurel expressed mixed opinions after the state selected their town as the site for a new $26.5 million forensic mental health facility funded by the 2025 Legislature under House Bill 5. While some residents supported the economic impact, others raised concerns about property values and the facility's proximity to West Elementary School and the Golf Course area.

headline: Laurel Police Officer Forced to Kill MHP K9 Dog During Attack in November 2024
summary: A Laurel Police officer was forced to end a dog attack in November 2024 after a canine police dog belonging to the Montana Highway Patrol attacked a man in the 800 block of Wyoming Avenue. The officer and the Laurel Volunteer Fire Department chief responded but were unable to stop the attack with non-lethal methods before the officer was forced to act.

headline: Intermountain Laurel Clinic Provides Family Medicine Services to Yellowstone County Community
summary: Intermountain Health's Laurel Clinic – Family Medicine, located at 1035 1st Avenue in Laurel, provides primary care services to families in this Yellowstone County community just west of Billings. The clinic is staffed by family medicine physicians and nurse practitioners, offering primary care services including preventive, chronic, and acute care.

headline: Montana Selects Laurel for New $26.5 Million Forensic Behavioral Health Facility
summary: The Montana Department of Public Health and Human Services and Board of Investments selected Laurel as the site for a new 32-bed forensic mental health facility funded by $26.5 million appropriated under House Bill 5 from the 2025 Legislature. The facility is designed for those in the justice system awaiting mental health evaluations, addressing rising demand that has strained the existing facility in Warm Springs.
source_name: Montana Behavioral Health Facility Site Evaluation

headline: Laurel Health and Rehabilitation Center Offers Skilled Nursing and Memory Care in Laurel
summary: Laurel Health and Rehabilitation Center, a non-profit nursing home in Laurel, Montana, provides skilled nursing, long-term care, physical and occupational rehabilitation, and memory care services to the community. The facility serves elderly and post-acute patients from Laurel and surrounding Yellowstone County communities.

headline: RiverStone Health Expands Rural Clinic in Joliet, Serves Laurel-Area Families Through Mobile Operations
summary: RiverStone Health, the Federally Qualified Health Center serving Yellowstone County, secured an alternative location to continue serving patients in Joliet beginning December 2024, expanding its rural healthcare footprint near Laurel. RiverStone's school-based health centers, rural clinics, and Healthcare for the Homeless program serve roughly 60,000 visits annually across the county.

headline: RiverStone Health Celebrates 40th Anniversary as Montana's First Community Health Center
summary: RiverStone Health celebrated its 40th anniversary as Montana's first Community Health Center in 2024, as well as its 50th anniversary as Yellowstone County's public health department. Serving Laurel and the broader Yellowstone County region, RiverStone provides care to about 300 patients daily at its main campus at 123 South 27th Street in Billings.

headline: Laurel Montana Tennis Association Launches Non-Profit to Fund New Community Courts
summary: Tessa Jochim launched the Laurel Montana Tennis Association non-profit to raise funds for two new tennis courts in Laurel, addressing a gap in the community's recreational infrastructure. The initiative was presented to the Laurel City Council's parks committee, which was simultaneously tracking upgrades at Kiwanis Park, Russell Park, and the community's splash park.

headline: Laurel Locomotives Football Posts Strong 2025 Season With Multiple Blowout Wins
summary: The Laurel Locomotives football team won eight of their first ten games in the 2025 season, recording blowout victories over Glendive (46-6), Sidney (34-6), Lockwood (35-7), Lewistown (35-21), Livingston (49-0), Browning (50-0), and Havre (49-13). The Locomotives play home games at Laurel High School and are a perennial contender in Montana Class A football.

headline: Laurel High School Locomotives Compete Across Multiple MHSA Sports Year-Round
summary: Laurel High School's athletics program fields competitive teams in football, basketball, volleyball, golf, track and field, and other Montana High School Association sports. The Locomotives compete in Class A and draw strong community support in the refinery and rail town just west of Billings along the Yellowstone River.

headline: Laurel Locomotives Max Preps Rankings Track State-Level High School Sports Standings
summary: Laurel High School's Locomotives are tracked on MaxPreps for Class A Montana standings across football, basketball, and other prep sports, with the school recognized as a competitive program in Yellowstone County. Laurel athletics draws on a tradition of community support in the Yellowstone River corridor.

headline: Bozeman Health and Billings Clinic Expand Partnership with Equal Land Ownership at West Bozeman Campus
summary: Bozeman Health and Billings Clinic announced in October 2025 an expansion of their collaboration, agreeing to equal ownership of undeveloped land at the Wellness Way medical campus near I-90. The partnership builds on a successful co-location arrangement started in late 2024, with joint planning underway to increase outpatient clinical access and surgery center capacity over the next year.
